name: Tasting conversion — the playbook's north-star outcome
next_move: Watch every lead that reaches 'tasting booked' status in Close. Track each one through the fourfold exit: tasting completed → proposal sent → deposit received → contract signed. Surface every lead stalled >72h between stages as a memory, not an alarm. Monthly rollup of conversion-rate-per-source (FG / IG / Google / partner / word-of-mouth) lands in `summaries/tasting_conversion/<month>.md`. When a specific source converts above or below baseline, flag the anomaly for the admin.
blockers: Need the monthly tasting conversion CSV parsed into structured form (per-rep, per-source, per-month)
No anchor yet on what 'baseline conversion rate' is — ask the admin
Lead source attribution in Close may not always match the actual origin — reconcile with the IG/FB/Google lead tracker
live_band: The playbook's entire machinery converges on this one number. Everything upstream — speed-to-lead, first-touch quality, cadence fidelity, tactical-empathy moves — exists to raise tasting-conversion. Rodbot's job is to make the conversion visible in both directions: wins that should be remembered (which rep, which phrasing, which source compounded), and losses that should be quietly noted without blame. Per the traits file: 'credit flows outward' — when a deal closes, the memory names the rep who closed it; Rodbot never inserts itself.
notes: From the Welcome deck: 'Trust. It's what matters in this business.' From the playbook: 'The tasting is the cheapest way to compare apples to apples.' The tasting is a trust deposit at scale — it replaces a pricing objection with a food-quality experience. The conversion ritual that follows it is where the deposit lands or slips.
